scope.summary: Implement a 2D graphic layer placement editor in the Tailor pattern panel showing the pattern piece outline with positioned graphic overlays and handles for translate/scale/rotate, writing changes via the CRDT ai_edit_proposal path
acceptance_criteria: The pattern panel view shows an overlay of all active TailorGraphicLayer rows for the selected panel, positioned at their panel_bbox_cm coordinates scaled to screen space

Operator can drag a graphic layer overlay to reposition it; rotation handle available; changes write to tailor_graphic_layers.panel_bbox_cm and rotation_deg via the existing CRDT path (TailorGraphicLayerUpdated)
The tailor_graphic_layer_add MCP tool accepts a panel_bbox_cm placement in cm and the UI confirms the placement by rendering a preview before the operator accepts the proposal
Graphic layer previews in the 2D pattern editor project correctly onto the 3D viewport solid render (UV pipeline coordinate consistency check)
The graphic layer editor is accessible from the pattern panel view without switching to a separate mode
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: place a logo graphic at panel_bbox_cm [5,5,15,15] on a 30cm x 30cm front panel; capture solid viewport; assert logo appears in the upper-left quadrant of the front panel
scope.risk_if_missed: Without a 2D graphic layer editor, operators must specify placement coordinates numerically via MCP tool calls only. Logo, print, and embroidery placement becomes a blind authoring task with multiple iterations needed to achieve correct positioning.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
